meta.title: Village Square Welcome Tour
meta.summary: Authored dialogue scenes for the village-square newcomer chain.

config.title: Guard Captain Marcus Contracts
config.idleText: Kaela gave you the briefing. The rest is discipline. If no contract is open with me, then your next lesson is outside these walls.

available.promptText: Now you look like someone who can survive beyond the square. Ready to close the welcome tour and take your final reward?
available.acceptedText: Good. Take your shoulders, take your potions, and stop thinking like a tourist. The meadow is your real instructor now.
available.detailsText: Once this is settled, the meadow stops being a guided walk and starts being your responsibility. You'll fight, gather, and learn without me holding the line for you.
available.freeformPrompt: Tell Marcus how you want to proceed.
available.freeformPlaceholder: Example: I'm ready. Finish the tour and send me out.
available.freeformFallback: Marcus studies you for a beat. "Either finish the tour or ask a clear question."

active.promptText: You're standing at the end of the welcome chain already. Report cleanly and we'll settle your final square reward.
active.resultText: Good. Take your shoulders, take your potions, and stop thinking like a tourist. The meadow is your real instructor now.
active.declineText: Then don't linger. Come back when you're ready to be judged by the field instead of the square.

stages.completedText: You've had your square tour and your final blessing. Whatever comes next, earn it in the field.

active.promptText: Back already. If you came to report in properly, we can finish your welcome here.
active.resultText: Good. Grimwald waits near the market and he owes every new arrival a practical start. Hear him out and keep moving.
active.secondaryLabel: What exactly was this meant to teach?
active.secondaryText: That first stop teaches the chain of command. You take an order, carry it through the square, and return ready for field work.

stages.completedText: You've handled my first introduction. Finish the rest of the square's tour, then return when you're actually equipped for a fight.

config.title: Grimwald's First Bargain
config.introLine: Ah, Marcus sent fresh boots my way. Good. A new arrival with a weapon is a customer who lives long enough to come back.
config.detailLine: This is simple: take Grimwald's bargain, hear the market's lesson, and walk away with steel worth more than your current luck.
config.freeformText: Ask, but make it worth the breath.
config.freeformPrompt: Tell Grimwald what you want from the bargain.
config.freeformPlaceholder: Example: Marcus sent me. What's the deal?
config.freeformFallback: Grimwald smiles thinly. "That wasn't a bargain, friend. Try again with intent."
config.completionLine: There. Steel in hand and a market lesson besides. Bron at the anvil has the next piece of your kit waiting.
config.revisitLine: We've already done business. If you're still wandering the square, Bron should be your next stop.
config.idleLine: No new bargain from me just now. The square still has work for you elsewhere.

config.title: Bron's Forge Handoff
config.introLine: If Grimwald sent you, then hold still and listen. Gear matters more than confidence, and confidence burns faster.
config.detailLine: The point is the vest, not the ceremony. Put proper leather on your chest before the meadow teaches you why iron alone is not enough.
config.freeformText: Speak over the hammer if you must.
config.freeformPrompt: Tell Bron what you need from the forge.
config.freeformPlaceholder: Example: I'm here for the kit you set aside.
config.freeformFallback: Bron snorts. "Use forge words, not fog. Ask clearly or move aside."
config.completionLine: Take the vest and stop looking half-finished. Thrain at the training ground has the next lesson, and he hates wasted motion.
config.revisitLine: Your forge stop is done. If your feet are still in the square, Thrain should be sharpening your habits next.
config.idleLine: I've no fresh forge work for you. Keep the chain moving.

config.title: Thrain's Training Counsel
config.introLine: So Bron has you armored enough to survive embarrassment. Fine. Then you can afford to hear what a real fight demands.
config.detailLine: Strength is not panic and bravery is not noise. The lesson is simple: stay planted, strike with intent, and stop confusing chaos for skill.
config.freeformText: If you have a question, ask it like a recruit who expects an answer.
config.freeformPrompt: Tell Thrain what you want to learn.
config.freeformPlaceholder: Example: Give me the lesson Marcus thinks I need.
config.freeformFallback: Thrain folds his arms. "Ask with a spine, recruit."
config.completionLine: Good. Keep the helm and the discipline. Herbalist Willow knows how to keep a body intact when steel alone won't save it.
config.revisitLine: You've had my lesson already. Willow is the wiser stop if you're still following the square's chain.
config.idleLine: I've said what I needed to say. Train or move on.

config.title: Willow's Forager Counsel
config.introLine: Thrain teaches how to hold the line. I teach how to last after the line breaks. Both matter more than pride.
config.detailLine: Every path beyond the square is made of things that can hurt you or heal you. Learn the difference early and the meadow becomes a teacher instead of a grave.
config.freeformText: Ask gently if you can. Plants answer patience better than force.
config.freeformPrompt: Tell Willow what you hope to understand.
config.freeformPlaceholder: Example: Thrain sent me. What should I notice out there?
config.freeformFallback: Willow tilts her head. "Slow down. Ask one thing clearly."
config.completionLine: Take the leggings and remember this: survival is often prepared long before danger appears. Chef Gastron has your next stop near the fire.
config.revisitLine: We've already spoken. If you still smell of the square, Chef Gastron is waiting with the next piece of your road kit.
config.idleLine: No fresh lesson from me right now. Follow the chain and keep your eyes open.

config.title: Gastron's Campfire Welcome
config.introLine: A warrior who forgets food and boots becomes a story told by someone better prepared. Sit, breathe, and take what I saved for you.
config.detailLine: Good boots and a fed body solve more problems than swagger ever will. This stop is about readiness that looks humble until it keeps you alive.
config.freeformText: Ask quickly before the stew turns on me.
config.freeformPrompt: Tell Gastron what you want to know.
config.freeformPlaceholder: Example: I'm on the square tour. What's your part in it?
config.freeformFallback: Gastron laughs once. "That question wandered off halfway through. Try again."
config.completionLine: Boots on, stomach settled, and still breathing. That's a promising start. Torvik in the mine mouth has the last square stop before Marcus judges you again.
config.revisitLine: You already have what I set aside. Torvik is the next voice worth hearing.
config.idleLine: Nothing new from the fire today. Keep the square's chain moving.

config.title: Torvik's Miner Briefing
config.introLine: If you made it this far, then the square hasn't broken your attention span yet. Good. The deep teaches harder lessons than the market.
config.detailLine: Mines reward patience and punish noise. Gloves, belt, and a bit of caution will carry you farther underground than courage alone ever will.
config.freeformText: Say what you mean. Rock has no patience for decoration.
config.freeformPrompt: Tell Torvik what you need from him.
config.freeformPlaceholder: Example: Gastron sent me. What should I take into the deep?
config.freeformFallback: Torvik grunts. "Try again without the extra dust."
config.completionLine: Take the gloves and belt. That's the last square courtesy you'll get. Marcus can decide whether you're finally ready for the meadow.
config.revisitLine: Your mining welcome is done. Marcus is the only stop left in this chain.
config.idleLine: I've no new contract for you. If you've finished with me, Marcus is the one who closes the circle.
